data.description:
## Problem
Codex epic execution treated inability to create a Gerrit feature/* branch as a hard blocker, even though the configured RebarBotNava credentials have successfully completed recent multi-child epics through dependency-ordered reviews pushed to refs/for/main. Current Codex guidance does not state this credential-aware fallback, while ADR 0025 warns the feature-branch interaction after the Rebase-If-Necessary cutover needs human review.

## Plan
- Add a concise Codex-specific rule to AGENTS.md beside the existing Codex environment guidance.
- Require checking current Gerrit history and configured identity capabilities before selecting a landing shape.
- When feature-branch creation is unavailable, use dependency-ordered refs/for/main changes with the normal LLM-Review + Verified gates; do not treat missing Create Reference rights as an implementation blocker.
- Keep the general multi-story guidance intact for authorized feature-branch drivers.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Codex guidance names the credential-aware refs/for/main fallback.
- [ ] The guidance preserves both required Gerrit votes and dependency ordering.
- [ ] The guidance cites the post-cutover feature-branch caveat rather than claiming all feature branches are obsolete.
- [ ] A documentation guard prevents accidental removal of the fallback.
data.title: Codex epic execution should fall back when feature-branch creation is unauthorized
